children.name: Ixonotus
children.description: Ixonotus — a genus of bulbuls in the family Pycnonotidae.

children.name: Ixonotus guttatus
children.description: The spotted greenbul or spotted bulbul, is a species of songbird in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae. It is classified in the monotypic genus Ixonotus. It is widely spread throughout the African tropical rainforest, from Sierra Leone and Guinea to Ghana; southern Nigeria to Uganda, northern Tanzania, eastern and central Democratic Republic of the Congo and extreme north-western Angola.

children.name: Ixos
children.description: Ixos — a genus of bulbuls in the family Pycnonotidae.

children.name: Ixos malaccensis
children.description: The streaked bulbul, or green-backed bulbul, is a songbird species in the bulbul family (Pycnonotidae). It is found on the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Borneo. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests.

children.name: Ixos mcclellandii
children.description: The mountain bulbul is a songbird species in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae. It is often placed in Hypsipetes, but seems to be closer to the type species of the genus Ixos, the Sunda bulbul. It is found in Southeast Asia and is not considered a threatened species by the IUCN.

children.name: Ixos nicobariensis
children.description: The Nicobar bulbul is a songbird species in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae. It is endemic to the Nicobar Islands. This species has sometimes been placed in the genus Ixos.

children.name: Ixos spec
children.description: Ixos spec — a species in the genus Ixos, family Pycnonotidae. Also known as bulbuls.

children.name: Ixos sumatranus
children.description: The Sumatran bulbul is a species of songbird in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae. It is endemic to Sumatra (Indonesia).

children.name: Ixos virescens
children.description: The Javan bulbul is a songbird species in the bulbul family. It is the type species of the genus Ixos. It is endemic to the island of Java in Indonesia in its natural habitat of subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.

children.name: Phyllastrephus
children.description: Phyllastrephus — a genus of bulbuls in the family Pycnonotidae.

children.name: Phyllastrephus alfredi
children.description: Sharpe's greenbul or the Malawi greenbul, is a species of songbird in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae. It is found in Africa in south-western Tanzania, north-eastern Zambia and northern Malawi.

children.name: Phyllastrephus cabanisi
children.description: Cabanis's greenbul, also known as Cabanis's bulbul, is a species of songbird in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae. It is found in east-central and south-central Africa. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical dry forest, subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest, subtropical or tropical moist montane forest, and subtropical or tropical moist shrubland.

children.name: Phyllastrephus poensis
children.description: The Cameroon olive greenbul, is a species of songbird in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae.

children.name: Phyllastrephus strepitans
children.description: The northern brownbul is a species of songbird in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae. It is found in eastern Africa. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical dry forests, dry savanna, and subtropical or tropical dry shrubland.

children.name: Phyllastrephus xavieri
children.description: Xavier's greenbul is a species of songbird in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae. It is found in central Africa. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical dry forests and subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests.

children.name: Pycnonotus
children.description: Pycnonotus — a genus of bulbuls in the family Pycnonotidae.

children.name: Pycnonotus barbatus
children.description: The common bulbul is a member of the bulbul family of passerine birds. It is widespread throughout most of Africa except for the very arid areas, and has recently begun breeding in southernmost Spain. Ten subspecies are recognised based on the geographical variation in plumage.

children.name: Pycnonotus brunneus
children.description: The Asian red-eyed bulbul is a member of the bulbul family of passerine birds. It is found on the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Borneo. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests.

children.name: Pycnonotus cyaniventris
children.description: The grey-bellied bulbul is a species of songbird in the bulbul family. It is found on the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra and Borneo. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests.

children.name: Pycnonotus hualon
children.description: The bare-faced bulbul is a member of the bulbul family of passerine birds. It was newly described from central Laos in 2009. It is one of the very few Asian songbirds with a bald (featherless) face and is the first new species of bulbul to be described from Asia in almost a century.

children.name: Pycnonotus jocosus
children.description: The red-whiskered bulbul, or crested bulbul, is a passerine bird native to Asia. It is a member of the bulbul family. It is a resident frugivore found mainly in tropical Asia.

children.name: Pycnonotus melanoleucos
children.description: #REDIRECT Black-and-white bulbul Category:Taxa named by Thomas Campbell Eyton.

children.name: Pycnonotus nigricans
children.description: The African red-eyed bulbul or black-fronted bulbul is a species of songbird in the family Pycnonotidae. It is found in south-western Africa. Its natural habitats are dry savanna, subtropical or tropical dry shrubland, and riverine scrub.

children.name: Pycnonotus priocephalus
children.description: The grey-headed bulbul is a member of the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae. It is endemic to the Western Ghats in south-western India, and found from Goa south to Tamil Nadu at altitudes up to 1200m. It is found in dense reeds or thickets mainly near rivers and swampy areas inside forests.

children.name: Pycnonotus sinensis
children.description: The light-vented bulbul, also called the Chinese bulbul, is a species of bird in the bulbul family found in Asia. A common species of songbird that favors lightly wooded habitats, it can frequently be seen in towns, suburbs and urban parks within its range.

children.name: Pycnonotus striatus
children.description: The striated bulbul is a species of songbird in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae. It is the only species placed in the genus Alcurus .

children.name: Pycnonotus tympanistrigus
children.description: The spot-necked bulbul is a species of songbird in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae. It is found in Sumatra. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical moist montane forest.

children.name: Andropadus
children.description: Andropadus — a genus of bulbuls in the family Pycnonotidae.

children.name: Chlorocichla
children.description: Chlorocichla — a genus of bulbuls in the family Pycnonotidae.

children.name: Chlorocichla falkensteini
children.description: Falkenstein's greenbul is a species of songbird in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae. It is found from western Cameroon and Central African Republic to central Angola. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests, moist savanna, and subtropical or tropical moist shrubland.

children.name: Chlorocichla laetissima
children.description: The joyful greenbul or joyful bulbul, is a species of songbird in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae. It is found in east-central Africa. Its natural habitats are boreal forests and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.

children.name: Chlorocichla prigoginei
children.description: Prigogine's greenbul is a species of songbird in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae. It is found only in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo.

children.name: Chlorocichla simplex
children.description: The simple greenbul or simple leaflove, is a species of songbird in the bulbul family of passerine birds. It is widespread throughout the African tropical rainforest. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical dry forests, subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests, and subtropical or tropical moist shrubland.

children.name: Malia
children.description: Malia — a genus of bulbuls in the family Pycnonotidae.

children.name: Malia grata
children.description: The malia (Malia grata) is a medium-sized (approximately 29 cm long) babbler-like passerine.

children.name: Hemixos
children.description: Hemixos — a genus of bulbuls in the family Pycnonotidae.

children.name: Hemixos castanonotus
children.description: The chestnut bulbul or chestnut-backed bulbul is a songbird in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae. The species was first described by Robert Swinhoe in 1870. It is found in southern China and northern Vietnam.

children.name: Hemixos flavala
children.description: The ashy bulbul is a species of songbird in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae. It is found on the Indian subcontinent and in Southeast Asia. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical moist montane forest.

children.name: Thescelocichla
children.description: Thescelocichla — a genus of bulbuls in the family Pycnonotidae.

children.name: Thescelocichla leucopleura
children.description: The swamp palm bulbul, is a species of songbird in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae. It is monotypic within the genus Thescelocichla.

children.name: Alophoixus
children.description: Alophoixus — a genus of bulbuls in the family Pycnonotidae.

children.name: Alophoixus pallidus
children.description: The puff-throated bulbul is a songbird in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae. The species was first described by Robert Swinhoe in 1870. It is found in Southeast Asia.

children.description: Neolestes — a genus of bulbuls in the family Pycnonotidae.
